name: Attention Mechanisms in Transformers
description: Core vocabulary behind how transformers focus on relevant parts of their input.

group.definition: A mechanism where each token in a sequence attends to every other token, letting the model capture long-range dependencies using **Query**, **Key**, and **Value** vectors.
A vector representing what a token is looking for, compared against **Key** vectors to determine relevance between tokens.
A vector representing what a token offers, dot-producted with **Query** vectors to compute **Attention Score**s.
A vector holding a token's actual content, combined via a **Weighted Sum** to produce the attention output.
A raw relevance score from the dot product of **Query** and **Key** vectors, turned into probabilities by **Softmax**.
Dividing **Attention Score**s by the square root of the key dimension before **Softmax**, preventing large values that destabilize gradients.
A function that normalizes **Attention Score**s into a probability distribution summing to one across all positions.
The output for a token, computed by combining **Value** vectors according to the probabilities from **Softmax**.
The ability of **Self-Attention** to process all sequence positions simultaneously, unlike the sequential computation of RNNs.
A shortcut that adds a layer's input to its output, preserving information and easing gradient flow through deep transformer stacks.
